theme_and_archetype.vibe: calm, luxury, sophisticated, warm, minimal, modern, natural, elegant, editorial, timeless
theme_and_archetype.comparable_brands: Cartier, Tiffany & Co., Michelin-star websites, Aman Resorts

color_palette.rules: Never use pure white (#FFFFFF) or pure black (#000000). Avoid bright yellow gold. All borders must be subtle and paper-colored.

rules.headings: Tracking-tight for large headings, generous line-height (leading-snug), elegant center-alignment for covers/specials, left-alignment for food lists.
rules.body: Tracking-normal, leading-relaxed, text-sm to text-base for descriptions.
rules.labels: Uppercase, tracking-[0.2em], text-xs, text-muted for categories and tags like 'CHEF RECOMMENDS'.

layout_and_spacing.container: Max-width centered, constrained height to simulate a real physical book. Deep padding around edges.
layout_and_spacing.spacing_scale: Generous. Use p-8, p-12, p-16 for structural padding. Create breathing room between menu items.
layout_and_spacing.menu_book_structure: A centralized book layout. Desktop: Open book showing 2 pages. Mobile: Single page visible. Absolute positioning for pages to layer them.

entrance_sequence.description: Ambient luxury restaurant background. Floating particles (slow motion). Center logo fades in softly. A delicate gold line expands underneath. Book container rises from bottom smoothly. 'Open Menu' elegant button appears.
entrance_sequence.interactions: Clicking 'Open Menu' triggers the cover to open, revealing the first page.

menu_book.description: A 3D perspective container. Pages rotate on the Y-axis (transform-origin: left). Use spring physics for the flip.
menu_book.page_design: Minimal. Dotted leaders or wide whitespace separating food name and price. No food thumbnails in the list, purely typographic.

food_detail_modal.description: Triggered by tapping a food item. The menu behind fades/blurs. A premium card floats up.
food_detail_modal.content: Large cinematic food photo at top, elegant typography for name, ingredients, veg/non-veg icon, prep time, spice level. Soft shadows.

navigation.description: Book-inspired UI. Ribbon bookmarks or subtle edge tabs to skip directly to Main Course, Desserts, etc. Elegant search overlay.

images.description: Luxury warm light restaurant interior for entrance background
Subtle paper texture overlay for the menu pages (use with mix-blend-mode: multiply and low opacity)

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. FRAMEWORK: Use React + Tailwind CSS + Framer Motion. Use .jsx/.js extensions exclusively. NO TypeScript (.tsx or .ts).
2. SETUP: Install 'framer-motion', 'lucide-react'. Import 'Cormorant Garamond' and 'Manrope' from Google Fonts in index.css.
3. CONFIG: Extend tailwind.config.js with the custom colors provided above (primary_background, secondary_background, accent_gold, text_main, text_muted).
4. ENTRANCE: Build a cinematic Entrance sequence. Render absolute floating particles. Animate the 'Empire' logo fading in, followed by a 'Open Menu' CTA.

5. 3D BOOK: Create an interactive Book component. Use a container with `perspective: 1500px`. Pages must have `transform-style: preserve-3d`, `transform-origin: left center`, and absolute positioning. Rotate Y-axis via framer-motion to flip pages.

6. CONTENT: Create a robust JSON-like data structure in JS containing the 17 requested categories. Populate it with fictional premium food items. Use the media URLs provided here for the detail modal.

7. UI ELEMENTS: Implement an elegant expandable Search and Veg/Non-Veg filter in the UI (perhaps as an overlay or sidebar). Use ribbon bookmarks on the right edge of the book for quick category jumping.
8. TESTING: Apply `data-testid` to all interactive elements (buttons, inputs, menu items, tabs, close buttons) in kebab-case format.
9. AESTHETIC: Mix paper_texture image over the background using `mix-blend-mode: multiply` and `opacity: 0.1` to simulate physical paper. Add subtle drop shadows to the book to give it weight.
